title: My God, My God, O Why Have Your Forsaken Me?

verses.text: Our fathers put their trust in you; From you salvation came. They begged you and you set them free; They were not put to shame. But as for me, I am a worm And not a man at all. To men I am despised and base; Their scornings on me fall. The Book of Psalms for Singing, 1973; alt

All those who look at me will laugh and cast reproach at me. Their mouths they open wide; They wag their heads in mockery. This man has trusted in the Lord; Let God redemption send. Now let his God deliver him, for he delights in him. The Book of Psalms for Singing, 1973; alt

You took me from my mother’s womb to safety at the breast. Since birth, when I was cast on you, You’ve been my God, my rest. Be not far off, for grief is near, And none to help is found. For bulls of Bashan circle me, Strong bulls do me surround. The Book of Psalms for Singing, 1973; alt

For see how dogs encircle me! On every side there stands A brotherhood of cruelty; They pierce my feet and hands. My bones are plain for me to count; Men see me and they stare. My clothes among them they divide, And gamble for their share. The Book of Psalms for Singing, 1973; alt

All those whose sould descend to dust Will fall before his throne; They cannot keep themselves alive, They rest on him alone. A seed shall serve him; future songs Will hear about the Lord. His righteousndess they will declare To people yet unborn. The Book of Psalms for Singing, 1973; alt
